metadata.title: Project Spec
metadata.purpose: This file, together with the other docs under harness/project-spec/, is the authoritative invariant space for harnessed work in this repo

project_is: A local Python/JSON coding-agent CLI harness.

A system that orchestrates clean-context coding agents through the OpenAI Agents SDK by treating role contracts, context packets, governance policies, output schemas, runtime reports, and acceptance evidence as explicit structured artifacts.
A harness exists to make agent-assisted software work inspectable, bounded, reproducible, and governed.

excluded_product_forms: A replacement for repo-specific project specs.
A framework for giving an LLM broad ambient access to files, shell commands, or tools.
excluded_agent_patterns: An agent "team" simulation where agents freely call each other.
A prompt collection where behavior depends on conversational momentum.
A hidden-memory system that treats prior chat as implementation authority.
project_is_not.design_guardrail: The harness does not try to make coding agents more magical. It tries to make their participation in software work more explicit, auditable, and bounded.

core_runtime_shape: A CLI entrypoint receives a user task.
The orchestrator selects a bounded workflow.
The context compiler produces a structured context packet.
Role contracts define what each agent is allowed to do and how it should interpret the packet.
The OpenAI API is used for bounded semantic transformation calls.
Report schemas define machine-checkable output shapes.
Runtime probes and saved artifacts distinguish scaffold claims from live-wired behavior.
Worker adapters are isolated from governance roles and require explicit authorization before file mutation.
The system is a governance runtime first, not an autonomy demo.

delineate_boundaries: Chat memory versus repo-local implementation state.
instantiate_governance: Controlling what context enters each agent call.
Separating role semantics from runtime mechanics.
Forcing structured reports instead of vague advice.
Recording evidence of what actually happened.
Preventing implementation from being treated as admissible merely because an agent wrote a plausible plan.
Keeping review and proof tied to repo-local artifacts, not chat history.

harness_must_respect: The current filesystem state of the repo.
The current git state where available.
The structured role contracts under harness/agents/**
Explicit user approval for approval-boundary crossings.

desired_user_outcomes.primary_operator: Primary operator is the project authority using agent assistance to build software wanting stronger control over context, authority, permissions, and evidence than a normal chat interface or IDE extension provides.

expectations: Compile repo-local context into a structured context packet.
Call the Project Manager route through the OpenAI Agents SDK.
Validate that report against the schema.
Save the task brief, context packet, and report under harness/runs/<timestamp>/

do_not_build: A vector database or RAG system.
Automatic repo mutation from a planning command.
Broad shell/tool access for governance agents.
Model-provider abstraction beyond what is needed for the first OpenAI SDK path.
do_not_fake: Scaffolding, fixtures, mocks, dry runs, or structure alone.
do_not_smooth_over: Ambiguity, uncertainty, or missing evidence with polished wording. The system must preserve uncertainty.
do_not_preserve_without_approval: Compatibility shims for earlier scaffold layouts unless explicitly approved.
Legacy behavior, donor-code assumptions, or compatibility shims unless explicitly required.

required_evidence_boundaries: Project spec versus current user task.
Model-generated claim versus deterministic probe result.
Archived history versus active implementation state.

required: OpenAI Agents SDK or OpenAI API call path.
OPENAI_API_KEY or equivalent configured outside committed files
excluded: No network tools other than the model API call.

first_slice_runtime_constraints: The first working implementation may be Python-only.
governance_representation_constraints: The first durable representation of governance should be JSON-first where the source is object-shaped.
forbidden_surfaces: Treating archived bundles as active state.
Treating missing optional sources as if they were present.
Claiming runtime capability without runtime evidence.
deferred_surfaces: Cost/budget enforcement beyond a first budget policy.
Provider abstraction beyond the first OpenAI route.
Editing repo files through an agent.
Allowing an agent to inspect files directly.

must_exist: Sending the structured packet through the PM route.
Saving required artifacts under a timestamped run directory.
Running a deterministic probe over those artifacts.
meaningful_progress_metrics: Clean transition from plan to later apply.

ux_properties: Avoid verbose terminal dumps by default.
Save detail to artifacts rather than hiding it in terminal scrollback.
runtime_expectations: Context compilation should be deterministic and grounded.
The orchestrator should be explicit rather than agent-directed.
The first workflow should be hardcoded enough to inspect: plan -> Project Manager -> validate -> save -> probe -> stop.
Later workflow expansion may add planner/adversary/reviewer/worker/archivist routes, but those routes must be explicit and approval-gated.
Every claimed runtime behavior must have a named acceptance probe.
operator_expectations: LLM reports do not substitute for deterministic artifact checks.
The harness should help the operator avoid accidental autonomy, hidden context contamination, and unreviewed file mutation.
inspectability_properties: Support novice inspection without requiring knowledge of hidden chat context.

acceptance_probes.question: Can the harness run the first PM planning slice from the CLI using the JSON agent contract, structured context packet, structured report validation, saved artifacts, and deterministic probe evidence?
not_proof: A mocked report without runtime route evidence.
A successful API call that does not save artifacts.
Saved artifacts that do not validate.
A PM report that claims the slice works without deterministic probe evidence.
A run that invokes planner, adversary, reviewer, implementer, archivist, or worker execution.

material_decisions: Whether role contracts should remain strict JSON or allow JSONC.
Whether the runtime should call the OpenAI Agents SDK specifically or use the lower-level OpenAI API client first.
Whether schema validation should use JSON Schema directly, Pydantic, or both.
Whether historical Markdown context packet artifacts should be retained only as archived run evidence or converted during archive cleanup
Whether budget policy enforcement belongs in the first or second vertical slice.
Whether planner/adversary/reviewer routes should be introduced before worker adapters.
clarifying_questions.material_for_probes: The safe early probe corpus is this repository's own harness files.
external_call_privacy_constraints: API calls use billing and must remain bounded.
Web search is disabled by default.
Agents may not run shell commands directly.
The plan workflow must not mutate repo source files except for saving run artifacts.
